LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER ANGIE CHRISTENSEN'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND DECLARATORY RELIEF IS GRANTED. THE COURT DENIES PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR TRADITIONAL MANDAMUS. PETITIONER'S REMEDY IS TO PURSUE ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS GIVEN THE UNDERLYING HEARING. IN ADDITION, THE STATUTORY LANDSCAPE REGARDING THE EXEMPTION WAS UNCLEAR AND PETITIONER DOES NOT CITE A CLEAR MINISTERIAL STATUTORY DUTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHANIE E. HAFFNER OF THE WESTERN CENTER ON LAW & POVERTY, TEL. NO. (213)235-2617 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. HOPE NAKAMURA OF THE LEGAL AID SOCIETY, TEL. NO. (650)558-0915 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. JENNIFER A. BUNSHOFT, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5085 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER ANGIE CHRISTENSEN'S Motion For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief is granted. First, California Welfare and Institutions Code sec. 11451.5 and AB 1542 do not appear to be an exhaustive list of what matters are exempt in calculating income. Second, respondent's position is contrary to its own definition of income. Respondent's regulation (sec. 44-101) provides that "income" is a cash benefit that is "currently available to the individual or is received by him." The income "must, in fact, be currently available to needy members of the family in meeting their needs'" Here, the child support that is transferred to children that live outside the home is not available to needy members of the family. Welfare and Institutions Code sec. 11450.12(b) similarly refers to "reasonably anticipated income." Third, respondent's interpretation of the governing scheme is contrary to Welfare and Institutions code sec. 11005.5. Double counting should not occur among recipient groups. In Rogers v. Dietrich (1976) 58 Cal.App.3d 90, 101, the court aptly explains: "To treat one person's aid as a reason to deny eligibility or to reduce assistance to which another is entitled amounts to defiance of the legislative proscription." The court denies petitioner's request for traditional mandamus. Petitioner's remedy is to pursue administrative mandamus given the underlying hearing. In addition, the statutory landscape regarding the exemption was unclear and petitioner does not cite a clear ministerial statutory duty. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief
